{{Dialogue|Carith|What is it?|Ketcher|I don't know. But I'm keepin' it.|Alimet|You'll get in trouble, you know. They told us to hand over anything unusual. That looks like something valuable.|Ketcher|Right. And I'm keepin' it.|attr=Ketcher discusses his discovery with Carith and Alimet|src=<ref name="TF:OSG" />}}
'''Ketcher''' was a [[Flight officer/Legends|flight officer]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Ketcher was stationed on the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Fogger]]'', where he befriended his fellow pilots [[Carith]], [[Alimet]] and [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]]. The group was later transferred to the [[Nebulon-B2 frigate]] ''[[Ludwick]]'', serving under [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] during a campaign against the [[Pakuuni Pirates]]. While being punished for an unauthorized trip to the ''Ludwick''{{'s}} [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] by helping to clean up a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] base, Ketcher found a small, red [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]], which he decided to keep for himself. After finding the crystal, however, Ketcher started to become lethargic and increasingly isolated. He died in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], within months of finding the crystal.

==Biography==
Ketcher joined the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] some time prior to the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. Around the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] victory over the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] at [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]<ref name="TF:OSG" /> in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Ketcher was serving as a [[TIE pilot]] aboard the [[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate]] ''[[Fogger]]'' and held the rank of [[Flight officer/Legends|flight officer]]. He formed a close friendship with two of his fellow pilots, [[Alimet]] and [[Carith]], known to the others as "Grommet", and the three spent much of their free time together, playing [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] or carrying out practical jokes. During a series of Imperial attacks following the Battle of Hoth that culminated in the destruction of the Rebel [[MC40a light cruiser/Legends|MC40a light cruiser]] [[Lulsla (MC40a light cruiser)|''Lulsla'']], the three met the future [[Ace/Legends|ace]] pilot [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]], who was then still a [[Cadet/Legends|cadet]] and newly transferred to the ''Fogger''. Stele went on to become a friend of Ketcher and the other pilots.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

[[File:Stele-headshot.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Ketcher's friend, Maarek Stele]]
Shortly after the ''Lulsla'' was destroyed, Stele was transferred to the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Protector (Victory I-class)|''Protector'']], leaving the others on the ''Fogger''. During this time, Ketcher and his friends survived many close calls, and Carith was promoted to [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]]. The group was later reunited with Stele, who had since been promoted to [[Captain/Legends|captain]], when all four found themselves assigned to the [[Nebulon-B2 frigate]] ''[[Ludwick]]'', as part of [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn's]] fleet in the [[Pakuuni system/Legends|Pakuuni system]].<ref name="TF:OSG" />

Thrawn was charged with establishing a new Imperial base in the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. The [[Battle on the Frontier|campaign]] started when the vice admiral took his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''Victory''-class Star Destroyer [[Stalwart (Victory-class)|''Stalwart'']], to [[Argoon]] to retrieve the parts and equipment needed to establish the base. Stele was charged with identifying which [[Cargo container/Legends|cargo containers]] held the equipment, so that they could be retrieved for delivery to the ''Stalwart'', but the operation was placed in jeopardy by the arrival of Rebel forces.<ref name="SW:TF">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref> Ketcher was one of several Imperial pilots who fought to repel the Rebel attack.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

During this tour of duty, Carith convinced his friends to accompany him on an unauthorized trip to the ship's [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] during a visit by Thrawn. The group had intended to take some [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] out for an unscheduled flight, but upon seeing Thrawn, just watched from behind some [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]] as the vice admiral explained their mission to the ''Ludwick''{{'s}} commanding officer, [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Buckeye]].<ref name="TF:OSG" /> Thrawn dispatched the ''Ludwick'' to destroy a [[Pakuuni Pirates|Pakuuni Pirate]] base to clear the way for the new Imperial facility.<ref name="SW:TF" /> Ketcher performed well in the mission,<ref name="TF:OSG" /> as Imperial fighters destroyed several cargo containers, [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] starfighters, and the [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvettes]] ''[[Leach]]'', ''[[Clavier]]'', and ''[[Grapler]]''.<ref name="SW:TF" />

After the destruction of the Pakuuni Pirate base, Commander Buckeye learned that a group of pilots had infiltrated the hangar during his meeting with Thrawn. It did not take him long to work out who, and he punished the group by sending them to the wrecked pirate base to help clean up and salvage any parts ahead of the arrival of the new Imperial base equipment. The group knew their punishment could have been worse—Buckeye had gone easy on them due to their recent performances—and enjoyed the opportunity to spend some time off the ''Ludwick''.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

[[File:Ludwick_mfrg.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Ketcher was stationed aboard the ''Ludwick''.]]
The pilots found little of interest at the pirate base, but among the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] and [[Transport/Legends|transport]] parts, Ketcher found a small, red [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] that caught his eye. He showed it to the others, who felt that he should turn it over to his superiors, but deciding that it looked valuable, Ketcher decided to keep it for himself and swallowed it to prevent it from being confiscated. After the crystal exited his body, he kept it on a small wire around his neck.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

Work on the new Imperial station proceeded quickly and, before long, station [[NL-1]] was nearing completion. Thrawn was due to arrive in the ''Stalwart'' to conduct an inspection of the facility, and the ''Ludwick''{{'s}} pilots were assigned to protect the vice admiral while his [[Delta-class DX-9 stormtrooper transport|transport]] traveled to the station and back to the ''Stalwart''.<ref name="SW:TF" /> Ketcher, along with his friends, was summoned to a briefing by Buckeye on the upcoming mission.<ref name="TF:OSG" /> With most of the ship's [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]] undergoing repairs after recent missions, the pilots were forced to use a combination of TIE interceptors and TIE bombers. Nevertheless, they were successful in fending off attacks by both Rebel and Pakuuni forces until Thrawn returned safely to the ''Stalwart''.<ref name="SW:TF" />

Over the coming weeks, Ketcher began to increasingly keep to himself. After a mission for which he was not on the active roster, the others decided to visit him in his quarters but found him looking listless, and he barely acknowledged their presence. Worried about him, the group attempted to talk him into a game of sabacc to cheer him up, but Ketcher assured them that he was fine and just needed to sleep. It would be the last time Stele would see his friend alive.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

Ketcher died at some point between this tour of duty and the completion of the [[TIE/d "Defender" Multi-Role Starfighter/Legends|TIE/D Defender]] prototypes by [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]]. Stele was transferred off the ''Ludwick'' prior to Ketcher's death, but the news was eventually relayed to him by Alimet, and both of them briefly mourned the loss of their comrade.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

==Personality and traits==
Ketcher was a tall man who was just under the maximum height limit for an Imperial TIE pilot. His height meant that he tended to stand with his shoulders forward and head craning, a pose that Stele thought made him look like he had trouble hearing. He had pale skin, reddish brown hair and thin lips while his hands were long, thin, and appeared to Stele to be slightly deformed. Despite his height, Ketcher was considered by Commander Buckeye to be one of his best pilots.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

He was part of a close-knit group of friends with Stele, Alimet, and Carith. The group spent most of their time together, though Ketcher was not the energetic type and preferred to play sabacc, a game at which he was known for his bluffing skill. The group was known to play jokes on the crew. On more than one occasion, Carith would make the call of a [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] outside the officer's mess, and the group would run and hide while their superiors came out to investigate. It was while doing this that they met Stele, when the pilot covered for them and said he'd seen an escaped animal running past.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

However, Ketcher was the most solitary of the group. When Stele came to say goodbye after being transferred for the first time, the others gave him a fond farewell, while Ketcher hardly looked up from the [[Holodrama/Legends|holofilm]] he was watching. After discovering the crystal, Ketcher had little energy and became increasingly isolated, making little effort to see his friends.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ketcher was created by [[Rusel DeMaria]] for the [[1994]] book ''[[TIE Fighter: The Official Strategy Guide]]'', which presented the story of Maarek Stele, the player character in the game [[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]. In the book, Ketcher is established as a friend of Stele's and identified as one of the previously unidentified pilots of the non-player [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] in the game. During the character's introduction, his name is spelled "Ketchel," however it is spelled "Ketcher" throughout the rest of the book. Since this is spelling used most frequently, it is assumed to be correct.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

The nature of the crystal found by Ketcher at the Pakuuni Pirate base is left vague in the book, as are the details of his death. However, the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] Stele sensed a dangerous power about the crystal when Ketcher first found it.<ref name="TF:OSG" />

*[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']] {{1st}} {{C|Starfighter only}}
